Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: Main power generation node for southern Cyprus. Loss causes island-wide blackout. Requires perimeter radar and steel barriers. MANPAD deployment recommended against air threats.
Primary power source for western Cyprus. Coastal proximity exposes it to seaborne attack. Requires coastal surveillance and missile interdiction system.
Strategic energy node on British Sovereign Base boundary. Close to NATO infrastructure. Requires coordinated defense protocol and backup feed line.
Gas storage and distribution hub. High explosion risk. Requires 500m security perimeter and drone defense system. CBRN team must be on standby.
Renewable energy capacity. Wide area makes physical protection difficult. Perimeter cameras and drone patrol recommended.
Largest reservoir in Cyprus. Chemical attack or sabotage could trigger island-wide water crisis. Chemical sensor network and underwater barrier mandatory.
Primary water source for Nicosia region. Sabotage causes capital water cutoff. Requires access control checkpoint and barrier system.
Capital drinking water passes through this facility. Chemical agent injection could affect entire city. CBRN sensor and personnel security screening mandatory.
Critical during drought periods. Requires backup power and cybersecurity measures against grid disruption.
Main civilian airport and potential evacuation hub. Requires perimeter radar and air defense system. Must be convertible to military operations in crisis.
Gateway to western Cyprus. Proximity to British Akrotiri base increases strategic value. Requires coordinated NATO defense plan.
Main trade and maritime export gateway. Requires underwater barrier and sonar monitoring. Primary target for hostile naval forces.
Secondary port. Becomes critical as supply line during potential blockade. Requires backup defense plan and mine countermeasure team.
Primary radar facility scanning Eastern Mediterranean. Loss creates blind spot. Requires backup radar and EMP protection.
NATO critical SIGINT intercept center. Top priority target. Mountain location advantageous but SHORAD air defense must be deployed.
National communications infrastructure center. Compromise severs command chain. Requires backup satellite link and offline protocol.
Land connection point for undersea cables. Physical sabotage can completely sever communications. Alternative cable route and satellite backup mandatory.

British Royal Air Force base. Typhoon aircraft and P-8 Poseidon sub-hunters based here. Most critical air power projection point in region. Air defense battery and perimeter security are top priority.
British sovereign base area and garrison. Provides strategic depth. Joint defense exercises with Cyprus National Guard required.
Main land forces base of Cyprus National Guard. Air defense systems and surface-to-surface missiles located here. Underground command bunker construction is priority.
Only naval base on the island. First line of defense against seaborne blockade. Fast attack boats and torpedo defense system must be deployed.
S-300 battery protecting the capital. Radar and missile silos must be concealed. Regular position rotation required.
Strategic weapons and ammunition depot. Secrecy of location is vital. Secondary depots must be created for target dispersion.
National command center. Attack renders government non-functional. Underground command bunker and evacuation tunnel mandatory.
Military decision-making center. Requires multi-layer security against cyber and physical attacks. Backup command center must be prepared.
Center for national intelligence operations. Location must remain classified. SIGINT and HUMINT capabilities must be integrated.
National crisis coordination hub. Requires backup power and communications for 30 days of autonomous operation.
Classified medical facility for combat casualties. Location known only to top command. Underground structure and CBRN capacity mandatory.
Main health facility in capital. Converts to military casualty reception during crisis. Backup power and CBRN decontamination unit mandatory.
National currency reserve and financial system hub. Requires strong security against cyber attack. Offline backup systems required.
Main base for F-16 and Rafale aircraft. Critical for Eastern Mediterranean air superiority. Air defense battery and S-300 system must be integrated.

Tesis Adı: Greece - Souda Bay NATO Naval Base
Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: One of NATO largest Mediterranean naval bases. Combined defense capacity with US and Greek fleets is critical. Primary departure point for reinforcement 280km from Cyprus.
Central air defense hub on NATO eastern flank. Patriot batteries and AWACS coordination managed here.
Strategic reserve air force base. HQ can relocate here under attack.
Main base of Greek naval forces. Proximity to Athens provides defense depth.
Radar network scanning Aegean and Eastern Mediterranean. Proximity to Cyprus adds strategic value for coordinated defense.
Greece largest port and strategic supply gateway. Deep defense plan required against naval blockade.
Closest NATO-controlled airport to Cyprus. Critical for reinforcement and evacuation operations in crisis.
Main port and logistics center of northern Greece. Critical as NATO supply line.
NATO supply and logistics node. Protection must be reinforced given increasing strategic importance.
Greece LNG import terminal. Critical link in energy security chain. Seaborne attack protection system must be installed.
Water source for greater Athens. CBRN monitoring and access control system required against chemical attack.
One of Mediterranean busiest ports. Regional supply and trade hub. Proximity to Suez route increases strategic value.

Tesis Adı: Egypt - Port Said Suez Canal Entrance
Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: Northern entrance of Suez Canal. Vital for global trade and energy flow. Blockage affects entire Mediterranean economy.
Gas export infrastructure. Directly linked to regional energy security. Critical within Cyprus EEZ agreement framework.
Egypt main air defense command center. Coordinated defense capacity critical for regional stability.
Closest Egyptian military airfield to Cyprus. Strategic value for coordinated operations in crisis.
Main point of Egyptian naval presence on western Mediterranean coast.
Main port point for Russian naval presence. Monitoring priority for NATO and Cyprus. Naval surveillance presence must be maintained.

Tesis Adı: Syria Coast - Tartus Russian Naval Base
Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: Russia only Mediterranean naval base. Directly affects regional power balance. 350km from Cyprus. SOSUS listening chain should cover this point.
Main base of Russian air forces in Syria. 370km from Cyprus. Su-35 presence threatens regional airspace. Continuous radar tracking and AWACS surveillance mandatory.
Syrian coastal radar network. Monitoring these systems provides early warning capacity toward Cyprus.
Malta airport in NATO member state at Mediterranean center. Strategic position for evacuation and supply operations.
Historic port and strategic maritime point. Hosts NATO naval presence. Naval defense system is critical.
Support point for NATO Mediterranean patrol operations. Critical for logistics and fuel resupply.
NATO most critical Mediterranean naval and air base. Center for P-8 Poseidon sub-hunters and Triton drone operations. Directly coordinates with Cyprus defense.
NATO Joint Forces Command South headquarters. Main node for Mediterranean defense planning. Directs Cyprus crisis scenarios.
Sicilian base for Italian and NATO naval forces. Critical for Mediterranean patrol and submarine defense.
Main base of US 173rd Airborne Brigade. Primary Mediterranean departure point for rapid reaction forces. Critical role in Cyprus evacuation scenarios.

Tesis Adı: Italy - Aviano US Air Force Base
Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: Base of US Air Force 31st Fighter Wing. Provides Mediterranean air support with F-16 aircraft.
Main Mediterranean base of French naval forces. Aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle based here. Critical for regional power projection.
France largest port and busiest in Mediterranean. Strategic supply and logistics center.
US Navy 6th Fleet Command. Coordinates all US naval operations in Mediterranean. Most critical support point in Cyprus crisis scenarios.
Largest US air base in Europe. Main base for power projection toward Mediterranean. Critical for Cyprus reinforcement operations.
Closest US Navy support base to Cyprus. P-8 Poseidon and Triton drone operations. 280km from Cyprus. Critical supply line.
US naval base at entrance to Mediterranean. Power transfer for Eastern Mediterranean crises conducted from here.
US Navy primary Mediterranean ISR hub. Hosts MQ-4C Triton and P-8 Poseidon for wide-area maritime surveillance.

Tesis Adı: East Med - Aphrodite Gas Field Block 12
Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: Cyprus largest offshore gas discovery. Naval protection and underwater security system mandatory. Corvette constant protection required.

Tesis Adı: East Med - Cyprus EEZ West Boundary
Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: Disputed maritime boundary. Continuous naval patrol and air surveillance required against unauthorized survey vessels.

Tesis Adı: East Med - Cyprus EEZ South Boundary
Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: EEZ southern boundary. Regular frigate patrol and satellite monitoring required.
Europe-Middle East undersea fiber optic cable network. Severing collapses regional internet infrastructure. Rapid repair vessel must be on standby.
Planned Cyprus-Greece-Italy energy corridor. Naval patrol must be incorporated into design against sabotage.

Tesis Adı: East Med - NATO Maritime Patrol Corridor
Detayli_Stratejik_Aciklama: NATO Eastern Mediterranean patrol route. Routine transit corridor for SNMG2 naval task group.
